2605.03231.title: cotomi Act: Learning to Automate Work by Watching You
2605.03231.paper_summary: What if a browser agent could learn your work simply by watching you do it? We present cotomi Act, a browser-based computer-using agent that combines reliable multi-step task execution with persistent organizational knowledge learned from user behavior. For execution, an agent scaffold with adaptive lazy observation, verbal-diff-based history compression, coarse-grained actions, and test-time scaling via best-of-N action selection achieves 80.4% on the 179-task WebArena human-evaluation subset, exceeding the reported 78.2% human baseline. For organizational knowledge, a behavior-to-knowledge pipeline passively observes the user's browsing and progressively abstracts it into artifacts (task boards, wiki) exposed through a shared workspace editable by both user and agent. A controlled proxy evaluation confirms that task success improves as behavior-derived knowledge accumulates. In our live demonstration, attendees interact with the system in a real browser, issuing tasks and observing end-to-end autonomous execution and shared knowledge management.
2605.03231.paper_summary_zh: 如果一個瀏覽器代理可以通過觀察你的工作來學習你的工作，那會怎樣？我們介紹 cotomi Act，一個基於瀏覽器的計算機使用代理，結合了可靠的多步驟任務執行和從用戶行為中學習的持久組織知識。為了執行，這個代理使用適應性懶觀察、基於語言差異的歷史壓縮、粗粒度動作和通過最佳選擇的 N 個動作進行測試時縮放，達到了 80.4% 的成績，超過了報告的 78.2% 人類基準。在組織知識方面，一個行為到知識的管道被動地觀察用戶的瀏覽，並逐步將其抽象為通過共享工作區暴露的文檔（任務板、維基），該工作區可由用戶和代理共同編輯。一項受控的代理評估證實，隨著行為衍生知識的積累，任務成功率提高。在我們的現場演示中，與會者在真實的瀏覽器中與系統互動，發出任務並觀察端到端的自主執行和共享知識管理。
2605.03231.author: Masafumi Oyamada et.al.
2605.03231.authors: Masafumi Oyamada, Kunihiro Takeoka, Kosuke Akimoto, Ryoma Obara, Masafumi Enomoto, Haochen Zhang, Daichi Haraguchi, Takuya Tamura
